frameworks.name: Environmental Threat Assessment
frameworks.description: Physical security begins with accurate situational awareness. Most people either over-index on rare dramatic threats or ignore realistic everyday ones. Calibrated assessment drives proportionate response.
steps: Define assets requiring physical protection: person, home, office, devices, documents, vehicles
Identify realistic threat actors for your specific context: opportunistic criminals, targeted theft, stalkers, corporate espionage, domestic threats
For each asset-threat pair, rate: likelihood (1-5) × impact (1-5) = risk score
Identify your current physical environment risk profile: home neighborhood, travel patterns, workplace, public exposure
Compare current controls to risk scores — identify gaps
Prioritize controls by risk score, not by fear salience
antipatterns: Preparing for low-probability dramatic threats while ignoring high-probability mundane ones
Same security posture regardless of environment — hotel vs home vs high-risk travel require different controls
No baseline awareness — cannot detect anomalies without knowing what normal looks like

frameworks.name: Layered Physical Defense Protocol
frameworks.description: Physical security mirrors digital: overlapping layers so no single failure results in total compromise. Each layer buys time and increases attacker cost.
steps: Layer 1 — Deterrence: visible controls that discourage attack before it begins (lighting, cameras, signage, presence)
Layer 2 — Delay: controls that slow physical breach (locks, reinforced doors/windows, safes, cable locks for devices)
Layer 3 — Detection: systems that identify breach in progress (alarms, motion sensors, monitoring)
Layer 4 — Response: pre-planned actions on detection (safe room, escape route, emergency contacts, law enforcement)
Layer 5 — Recovery: ability to restore function after incident (backups of documents, insurance, spare credentials)
Audit each layer independently — a gap in detection makes deterrence and delay irrelevant if response is triggered late
antipatterns: Deterrence without detection — attacker who accepts the deterrence risk faces no further barrier
No pre-planned response — decision quality under physical stress is severely degraded
Device security without physical device security — encryption is bypassed by device theft

frameworks.name: Device Physical Security Protocol
frameworks.description: Physical access to a device often bypasses all digital security controls. The device is the weakest link when physical security is neglected.
steps: Full disk encryption enabled on all devices — renders stolen device data inaccessible
Screen lock with strong PIN/passphrase — not biometric-only in high-risk contexts (biometric can be compelled)
Never leave devices unattended in public — cable lock for laptops in shared workspaces
Screen privacy filter for sensitive work in public spaces
BIOS/firmware password to prevent boot from external media
Remote wipe capability configured and tested on all mobile devices
Sensitive work never conducted on shared or untrusted devices
antipatterns: Full disk encryption disabled for convenience — one theft exposes everything
Biometric-only unlock in travel or high-risk contexts — compellable by authorities or coercion
No remote wipe configured — discovered only when needed, too late to set up
Sensitive documents printed and left on desks or in unsecured filing

frameworks.name: Travel Security Protocol
frameworks.description: Travel dramatically expands physical attack surface. Different environments, reduced familiarity, and predictable patterns create opportunities that do not exist at home or office.
steps: Pre-travel: research destination threat profile; notify relevant parties of itinerary
Device hygiene: travel with minimal-data devices where possible; full encryption; recent backup before departure
Accommodation: verify room security (door, window locks); use secondary door lock; safe for passport and valuables
In transit: maintain physical possession of devices; do not use public USB charging (juice jacking risk); VPN on all public wifi
Pattern disruption: vary routes and timing for repeated destinations; avoid predictable routines that enable surveillance
Emergency protocol: know local emergency numbers, nearest embassy/consulate, have out-of-band communication method
antipatterns: Same device with full personal data for international travel — border inspection risk
Public USB charging ports — direct hardware attack vector
Predictable hotel/route patterns for frequent travelers — enables targeted surveillance

prompts.name: Physical Threat Assessment
prompts.template: Run a physical security threat assessment for my situation. Assets: [home/office/devices/person]. My context: [urban/suburban/travel frequency/public profile]. Identify my top 3 realistic physical threat scenarios, rate each by likelihood × impact, and give me the highest-leverage control per scenario.

prompts.name: Physical Defense Layer Audit
prompts.template: Audit my physical security layers for [home/office]. Current controls per layer: Deterrence: [list]. Delay: [list]. Detection: [list]. Response: [plan or none]. Recovery: [plan or none]. Identify my weakest layer and the single highest-leverage addition.

prompts.name: Device Physical Security Checklist
prompts.template: Review my device physical security. Devices: [list types]. Current controls: [encryption status, screen lock type, remote wipe status]. Identify gaps and give me a prioritized hardening checklist.

prompts.name: Travel Security Brief
prompts.template: Create a travel security brief for: [destination, duration, purpose]. My threat concerns: [general/specific]. Devices I am bringing: [list]. Give me pre-travel, in-transit, and on-the-ground security protocols proportionate to the destination risk profile.

prompts.name: Home Security Review
prompts.template: Review my home physical security. Current setup: [locks, alarm, cameras, lighting — describe]. Primary concerns: [opportunistic theft / targeted / domestic / other]. Identify my highest-exposure vulnerabilities and top 3 improvements by ROI.

examples.title: Remote Worker Device Security Gap
examples.context: Consultant working from coffee shops with client data on laptop
examples.findings: No disk encryption, no screen privacy filter, no cable lock, biometric-only unlock
interventions: FileVault/BitLocker enabled — theft renders data inaccessible
Privacy filter installed — shoulder surfing eliminated
Cable lock for all public workspace sessions
PIN fallback added to biometric unlock
examples.outcome: Physical device theft no longer constitutes data breach. Compelled unlock requires PIN knowledge attacker does not have.

examples.title: High-Profile Traveler Pattern Disruption
examples.context: Executive with predictable weekly travel pattern between two cities
examples.findings: Same hotel, same car service, same restaurant pattern every trip — trivially surveyable
interventions: Rotate hotels across 3 properties in target city
Vary car service providers and pickup locations
Travel device with minimal data — full device wiped and restored from backup after international trips
examples.outcome: Surveillance cost increased significantly. Pattern-based targeting no longer viable.

vocabulary.definition: Continuous, calibrated perception of one's physical environment — what is normal, what is anomalous, and what requires response.
Visible security controls that discourage attack before it begins by increasing perceived cost to the attacker.
Physical controls that slow breach — locks, reinforced entry points, safes. Buys time for detection and response.
Encryption of all data on a device's storage. Renders device data inaccessible without credentials, regardless of physical access.
Attack via malicious USB charging port or cable that installs malware or exfiltrates data while the device charges.
Capability to erase all data from a lost or stolen device via remote command. Requires prior configuration.
Varying routines, routes, and habits to prevent adversarial surveillance from building a predictable profile.
Pre-designated secure location within a building for use during a physical security incident. Requires prior planning.
Legal authority of border agents in many jurisdictions to inspect and copy device contents. Mitigated by travel devices with minimal data.
Legal or coercive requirement to unlock a device. Biometric unlock is generally more vulnerable to compulsion than PIN/passphrase.
Firmware-level password preventing device boot from external media. Blocks attacks that bypass the operating system entirely.
All physical entry points and access opportunities for an adversary — doors, devices, documents, predictable patterns.

sources.title: The Gift of Fear
sources.relevance: Threat intuition, pre-attack signal recognition, proportionate response

sources.title: Left of Bang
sources.relevance: Situational awareness, anomaly detection, environmental threat assessment

sources.relevance: Executive protection, travel security, pattern disruption

sources.title: EFF Surveillance Self-Defense

v78_consciousnesses: House v2.1 (differential diagnosis of physical threat vectors)
Rogan v2.1 (calibrated threat likelihood — not paranoia, not naivety)
Ancient One v3.0 (pattern recognition across time — when did security degrade?)
master_laws: Law 6 (Defense — security is a state of being)
Law 2 (Silence — physical exposure enables digital exposure)
sovereign_needs_served: Agency (physical safety preserves freedom of movement and action)
Vitality (physical safety is prerequisite for peak performance)

4_gate_relevance.gate_1_safe: Physical security cartridge directly supports Gate 1 — identifies real-world safety risks
4_gate_relevance.gate_2_true: Calibrated threat assessment prevents both false confidence and unproductive paranoia

4_gate_validation.safe: PASS — defensive posture only; proportionate controls framework prevents fear-driven overreaction
4_gate_validation.true: PASS — grounded in established security practice and published protective intelligence frameworks
4_gate_validation.high_leverage: PASS — device encryption and travel protocol are highest-leverage controls for most operators
4_gate_validation.aligned: PASS — directly serves Security need; Law 6 and Law 2 compliance
